title: System and methods for physical whiteboard collaboration in a video conference
abstract: Embodiments are described for provision of video conferencing with, for example, a virtual whiteboard for displaying annotations and streaming of a physical whiteboard, indexing of physical whiteboard content, and compositing and projection of remote whiteboard content onto a local whiteboard.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: accepting, at a point of presence, from a plurality of participants to a video conference, a plurality of audio and video streams from a plurality of video-conference endpoints, each video-conference endpoint associated with at least one respective participant of the plurality of participants; accepting, at the point of presence, a first physical -whiteboard stream generated based on a sequence of images of a first physical whiteboard; enabling, at the point of presence, a virtual whiteboard comprising the first physical- whiteboard stream; and providing a representation of the appearance of the virtual whiteboard as a virtual- whiteboard stream to the plurality of video conference endpoints. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: indexing the first physical -whiteboard stream by associating handwritten or hand- drawn content with a plurality of corresponding recognized objects. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein indexing comprises saving unique recognized objects in an index file or as a saved-whiteboard object in a searchable database. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein indexing is performed in real time during the video conference. 5. The method of claim 3, further comprising providing the index file to the plurality of participants in the video conference after the conclusion of the video conference. 6. The method of claim 2, wherein recognized objects are text objects, geometric objects, or complex objects. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising: accepting, at the point of presence, a second physical-whiteboard stream associated 

 with a second physical whiteboard; and providing a first projector stream to a first projector endpoint associated with the first physical whiteboard, the first projector stream based on the second physical -whiteboard stream, wherein the virtual-whiteboard stream comprises a composite of the first physical- whiteboard stream and the second physical -whiteboard stream. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the first projector stream excludes visual content from the first physical -whiteboard stream. 9. The method of claim 7, the first projector stream further comprising one or more annotations associated with the virtual whiteboard, wherein the one or more annotations were created by a respective participant to the video conference using a graphical user interface on a computing device associated with a video-conference endpoint. 10. The method of claim 1, the virtual whiteboard further comprising one or more annotations associated with a two-dimensional location within a displayable area and a participant identifier. 11. A system, comprising: a processor; a storage device communicatively coupled to the processor; and a set of instructions on the storage device that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: accept, at a point of presence, from a plurality of participants to a video conference, a plurality of audio and video streams from a plurality of video-conference endpoints, each video-conference endpoint associated with at least one respective participant of the plurality of participants; accept, at the point of presence, a first physical -whiteboard stream generated based on a sequence of images of a first physical whiteboard; enable, at the point of presence, a virtual whiteboard comprising the first physical -whiteboard stream; and provide a representation of the appearance of the virtual whiteboard as a virtual-whiteboard stream to the plurality of video conference endpoints. 12. The system of claim 11, the instructions further comprising: index the first physical -whiteboard stream by associating handwritten or hand-drawn content with a plurality of corresponding recognized objects. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein indexing comprises saving unique recognized objects in an index file or as a saved-whiteboard object in a searchable database. 14. The system of claim 12, wherein the system is configured to perform indexing in real time during the video conference. 15. The system of claim 13, the instructions further comprising: provide the index file to the plurality of participants in the video conference after the conclusion of the video conference. 16. The system of claim 12, wherein recognized objects are text objects, geometric objects, or complex objects. 17. The system of claim 11, the instructions further comprising: accept, at the point of presence, a second physical -whiteboard stream associated with a second physical whiteboard; and provide a first projector stream to a first projector endpoint associated with the first physical whiteboard, the first projector stream based on the second physical -whiteboard stream, wherein the virtual-whiteboard stream comprises a composite of the first physical- whiteboard stream and the second physical -whiteboard stream. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the first projector stream excludes visual content from the first physical -whiteboard stream. 19. The system of claim 17, the first projector stream further comprising one or more annotations associated with the virtual whiteboard, wherein the one or more annotations were created by a respective participant to the video conference using a graphical user interface on a computing device associated with a video-conference endpoint. 20. The system of claim 11, the virtual whiteboard further comprising one or more annotations associated with a two-dimensional location within a displayable area and a participant identifier.
